Description: MAG: Cellvibrionaceae bacterium isolate 55a8bffa-a2ad-4596-9e63-39a54d4c2177 genome assembly, chromosome: 1
Taxonomy_y: Bacteria; Pseudomonadati; Pseudomonadota; Gammaproteobacteria; Cellvibrionales; Cellvibrionaceae
Assembly: The metagenome-assembled genome assembly wpAphLong1.Cellvibrionaceae_1.1 is based on 32x PacBio data generated by the Aquatic Symbiosis Genomics Project (https://www.aquaticsymbiosisgenomics.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with metaMDBG and coverage-, graph-, and HiC-based contig binning.

Taxonomy_x: Eukaryota; Metazoa; Ecdysozoa; Arthropoda; Hexapoda; Insecta; Pterygota; Neoptera; Endopterygota; Lepidoptera; Glossata; Ditrysia; Noctuoidea; Noctuidae; Oncocnemidinae; Calophasia
Taxonomy_y: Eukaryota; Metazoa; Ecdysozoa; Arthropoda; Hexapoda; Insecta; Pterygota; Neoptera; Endopterygota; Lepidoptera; Glossata; Ditrysia; Noctuoidea; Noctuidae; Oncocnemidinae; Calophasia
Assembly: The assembly ilCalLunu1.hap1.1 is based on 76x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Chromosome Z was assigned based on alignment to Trisateles emortualis (GCA_947095525.1). Putative B chromosome found which looks to be derived from two parts of two different autosomes corresponding to the ends of Chromosomes 19 and 22.

Assembly: The assembly icBarPell3.1 is based on 37x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size.
